title: eriogonum grande subsp. rubescens
intro: Eriogonum grande subsp. rubescens, also known as the red-stemmed buckwheat, is a subspecies of the Eriogonum grande plant, a type of flowering shrub native to the southwestern United States and Baja California. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds, and has been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The red-stemmed buckwheat has been found to offer health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and supporting the immune system. Herbal preparations of Eriogonum grande subsp. rubescens include teas, infusions, and topical creams, which can be made by drying and processing the plant's leaves and stems.
